--- a/app/channels/stimulus_reflex/channel.rb
+++ b/app/channels/stimulus_reflex/channel.rb
@@ -80,10 +80,14 @@
       arguments = data["args"] || []
       element = StimulusReflex::Element.new(data)
 
+      reflex_class = reflex_name.constantize
+      unless reflex_class.ancestors.include?(StimulusReflex::Reflex)
+        raise ArgumentError.new("#{reflex_name} is not a StimulusReflex::Reflex")
+      end
+
+      allowed_methods = reflex_class.ancestors[0...reflex_class.ancestors.index(StimulusReflex::Reflex)].flat_map { |a| a.public_instance_methods(false).map(&:to_s) }
+      raise ArgumentError.new("Reflex method '#{method_name}' is not defined on class '#{reflex_name}'") unless allowed_methods.include?(method_name)
+
       method = reflex.method method_name
       required_params = method.parameters.select { |(kind, _)| kind == :req }
       optional_params = method.parameters.select { |(kind, _)| kind == :opt }
```

*Note: Based on the description provided, the vulnerability exists because `reflex.method(method_name)` retrieves any method available on the Ruby object. By checking the `ancestors` of the reflex class up to `StimulusReflex::Reflex`, we restrict execution to only those methods intended to be reflexes.*

--- a/src/mrkdwn/escape.ts

 const escapeEntityMap = {
   '&': '&',
   '<': '<',
   '>': '>',
 }
@@ -21,7 +21,7 @@

-      (_, __, quote) => `${quote}\u200b${_.slice(quote.length)}`

+      (_, quote, firstChar) => `${quote}\u200b${firstChar}`
